devdoc.details: The agent (operator) is the owner. It pre-flights any action with the         `check` view, then calls `execute` to perform it. The contract is the         enforcement point, not the agent.

returns.ok: true if the action would be permitted right now

execute(address,uint256,bytes).details: Reverts identify which policy blocked the action, so a denied         attempt is still a legible on-chain event.

devdoc.title: PolicyGuard

check(address,uint256).notice: Returns the policy verdict for a proposed action without doing it.

execute(address,uint256,bytes).notice: Executes a proposed action only if it passes every policy.         The caller forwards the native value with the call (msg.value).

userdoc.notice: On-chain guardrail for an autonomous agent. Every action the agent         takes must route through `execute`, which enforces three policies         on-chain so they cannot be bypassed off-chain:           1. Allow-list — the target address must be explicitly permitted           2. Spend cap  — the native value of a single action is bounded           3. Pause      — the operator can halt all actions instantly
